content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: I accept the contribution because the solution correctly applies the 4–20 mA linear scaling formula and produces accurate results.

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: nice because nice

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: This is just the right difficulty to be done in 5 minutes.

Original & Very clean! I like it :)
Your “Temperature -100 to +100 °C” test uses negative numbers, but your validator does not. Ideally, the validator should test for exactly the same trap. How about something like -50 to 150 instead ?

Sections
* Please study the example on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and rewrite your statement properly as described in the points below.

Goal / Input / Output
* Refer to the above-mentioned example and rewrite the Input section in the same format.

* Refer to the above-mentioned example and rewrite the Output section in the same format.

* The Output section should include a simple description of the output only (in this clash, that should be just a single line). All task explanations should be moved to the Goal section.

Game modes
* Could it be too difficult or even impossible to guess the task in Reverse mode? If so, consider disabling that mode.

Cases
* Please remove the trailing newlines from the input of all the cases.

* Each test and its validator (one pair) should cover the *same scenario* with similar (but different) inputs and outputs. Please revise the cases accordingly. You may have more than 4 pairs of cases to cover more scenarios.

Thanks.

I'd tried to use realistic scenarios from industry. Temperature range wouldn't always be a negative to a positive. 5DN1L picked up on same thing. I'll do 2 temps, with one being neg to pos and the other both pos... hopefully that will be satisfactory. Thanks for input.

Another trap (for those who don't read the statement nor use the default code during a clash) is that inputs with a fractional part (37.5, 3.8) appear in validators only. Please include such inputs in two of the visible tests as well.

Thanks.
Ok, I've changed the 2 tests you referred to so they include decimals.
